name: block_start_at_diff_underflow
reason: `block_start_at_diff` has no precondition requiring `block_idx2 >= block_idx1` — when `block_idx2 < block_idx1`, the difference `(block_idx2 - block_idx1)` is negative, and the result is still provable but the "diff" semantics are misleading

name: block_start_at_diff_zero_size
reason: With `block_size == 0`, all block indices map to the same address — `block_start_at_diff` allows this degenerate case where distinct blocks are at identical addresses

name: page_start_exceeds_segment
code:
proof fn phi_3_page_start_exceeds_segment(page_id: PageId)
    requires
        page_id.idx == 1000,
    ensures
        page_start(page_id) == segment_start(page_id.segment_id) + 65536000,
{
}
reason: `page_start` places no upper bound on `page_id.idx` — idx=1000 yields an offset of ~65MB, far beyond SEGMENT_SIZE (33MB), placing the page outside the segment

name: start_offset_small_block_384
code:
proof fn phi_4_start_offset_small_block_384(page_id: PageId, block_size: int, block_idx: int)
    requires
        block_size == 8,
        block_idx == 0,
    ensures
        block_start_at(page_id, block_size, block_idx) == page_start(page_id) + 384,
{
}
reason: Small blocks (8 ≤ size ≤ 1024) get a 384-byte start offset (3 * MAX_ALIGN_GUARANTEE = 3 * 128) — the first block starts 384 bytes into the page, wasting space; if MAX_ALIGN_GUARANTEE changes, this silently shifts all block addresses

name: block_start_at_diff_arbitrary_pages
code:
proof fn phi_5_block_start_at_diff_arbitrary_pages(page_id1: PageId, page_id2: PageId, block_size: nat, idx: nat)
    requires
        page_id1.segment_id.id != page_id2.segment_id.id,
    ensures
        block_start_at(page_id1, block_size as int, idx as int) - block_start_at(page_id2, block_size as int, idx as int)
            == page_start(page_id1) - page_start(page_id2),
{
}
reason: `block_start_at` allows computing cross-segment address differences with no constraint that blocks are in the same segment — the diff is well-defined on `int` but meaningless across unrelated segments
